architecture: Components must be strictly separated into stateless (pure UI) and stateful (container/hook) logical segments.
Custom hooks must be extracted for any component logic exceeding 30 lines or handling side effects.
Global state should be avoided unless absolutely necessary; prefer Context API or component composition.
performance: Use React.memo ONLY if a component renders often with the exact same props and computing the render is expensive.
Memoize expensive derived state using useMemo.
Wrap stable callback functions passed as props to child components in useCallback.
Never define inline arrow functions inside JSX iterators (e.g., .map) or deeply nested child components.
typescript: Implicit 'any' is strictly forbidden. Interfaces or Types must be explicitly defined for all API responses, component props, and state.
Props interfaces must be named identically to the component suffixed with 'Props' (e.g., ButtonProps).
Avoid type assertions (e.g., 'as User') unless absolutely necessary; prefer type guards.
styling: Inline styles (e.g., style={{ color: 'red' }}) are strictly forbidden.
All dynamic UI manipulation must be handled via toggling CSS classes.
CSS Variable injection is acceptable for dynamic user-driven coordinates or colors but should be typed as React.CSSProperties.
clean_code: Magic numbers and strings must be extracted into a separate constants file.
Avoid deeply nested ternary operators in JSX. If a condition requires more than one logical branch, extract it to a helper function.
Dependency arrays in useEffect must be exhaustively declared. Do not disable exhaustive-deps lint rules.
eslint_enforcement: Code must be written as if strictly validating against 'eslint-plugin-react-hooks' (exhaustive-deps).
Code must pass 'eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y' for accessibility (e.g., passing alt tags to images, aria-labels to buttons).
Do NOT use 'eslint-disable-next-line' or '@ts-ignore' anywhere to bypass quality checks unless explicitly approved by the user.
Ensure all React imports (like memo, useState) are explicitly used; no unused imports or variables permitted ('no-unused-vars').
